title: The Glory of Light: Urim and Thummim, the Philosophical Stone of Light
subtitle: Transcribed by Adam McLean from MS. Ashmole 1415 — a treatise showing Urim and Thummim to be made by Art, the Universal Spirit corporate and fixed

summary.one_line: An alchemical treatise on the artificial production of Urim and Thummim as the fixed Universal Spirit — the spiritualization of matter and the materialization of spirit in a transparent, light-givi...
summary.scope: Full text of 'The Glory of Light' from MS. Ashmole 1415. Covers the relation of Urim and Thummim to the Ark of Noah, the Temple Veil, the two Lights, the three kinds of Gold (vulgar, chymical, divine), the squaring of the circle, and the central doctrine that corporeal must become spiritual and spiritual corporeal.

sources.label: The Glory of Light — transcribed by Adam McLean from MS. Ashmole 1415, ff.61-70

body.text: That Urim and Thummim were given on the Mount cannot be proved, yet they are potential from the Creation may appear, for they were substances whose name and essence did predicate each other being convertible terms: the name and essence one, the words signify Light and perfection, knowledge and holiness, also manifestation and truth; even as science and essence make one perfection. It is likely they were before the Law given, for the Almighty commanded Noah to make a clear light in the Ark, which some take for a window, others for the arching and bowing of the upper deck a cubit. Other Hebrew Doctors say it was a precious stone hung in the midst of the Ark, which gave light to all living creatures therein, this the greatest carbuncle could not do, nor any precious stone that is only natural, but the Universal spirit fixed in a transparent body shines like the Sun in glory, and gives sufficient light for all the room to unveil by. Therefore it is most probable this was the light that God commanded Noah to make.

The Sun and Moon are as the Parents of all inferior bodies and those things which come nearest in virtue and temperature are more excellent, The Sun's motion and virtue doth vivify all inferior bodies, and the pure form of the Terrestrial Sun is said to be all fire, and therewith doth the Celestial Sun communicate most virtue. Therefore the uncorrupted quality of pure l being digested in external heat hath also regal power over all inferior bodies, for the Sun doth infuse his influence into all things, but especially into Gold, and these natural bodies do now show forth their virtues, until they be made spiritual. Now that these perfections have their beginnings from two Lights, both the text and the ancient philosophers make plain, but ignorance and the matter of the elements are the Iron Gates which must be cut in pieces before the invisible be made visible.

Here is that rule made good. Except you sow gold in gold you do nothing. Therefore we must take heed what we understand by gold. Whereof there are 3 sorts — vulgar, Chymicall and Divine; which is therefore so called because it is a special gift of God. The Theosophists are persuaded by exact diet, and by certain forms of prayers, at certain times to obtain the Angel of the Sun, to be their guide and director. This is the king that made the pure (Thummim) clear bright fountain, and of it was made himself, the fair Woman so loving the red man, she became one with him, and yielded him all glory, who by his regal power and sovereign quality reigneth over the fourfold nature eternally, but if any shall understand either common or chemical gold to be the subject of this sacred body, he is much mistaken for a glorious spirit will not appear save in a body of his own kind.

The 'Universal spirit fixed in a transparent body' that 'shines like the Sun in glory' is the alchemical description of what the Layayoga tradition calls the 'shining body' (prakasha-deha) or 'divine body' (divya-deha), attained when kundalini reaches the sahasrara and the practitioner's pranic vehicle becomes permanently luminous. This is identical to the 'augoeides' (radiant body) of Iamblichean theurgy — the vehicle of the soul that is purified and 'fixed' through the rites (PGM IV.475-829). Steiner describes this as the 'astral body transformed into a light-body' through imaginal cognition. The treatise's claim that Urim and Thummim are 'convertible terms' with 'Light and perfection' references the syzygy of illumination and stability that the Tantraloka describes as prakasha (light) and vimarsha (reflective awareness) — the two poles of consciousness whose union produces the 'supreme light' (para-prakasha). The 'Iron Gates' that 'must be cut in pieces' are the granthis (knots) of the subtle body that block the ascent of kundalini.

The 'two Lights' from which 'perfections have their beginnings' are the solar and lunar currents (pingala and ida) that govern the subtle physiology. The statement that 'the Sun doth infuse his influence into all things, but especially into Gold' describes the sushumna's position between ida and pingala: the central channel receives the essences of both lateral currents. The 'pure form of the Terrestrial Sun is said to be all fire' corresponds to the manipura chakra's tejas-tattva, the fire element that is the transformer of all inferior (grosser) tattvas. The process of making bodies 'spiritual' until they 'show forth their virtues' is the same as the Layayoga practice of bhuta-shuddhi and tattva-shuddhi, where each element is resolved into its subtle principle. In Steiner's account, this is the 'alchemical transformation of the etheric body' that occurs when the practitioner learns to 'think in the element of fire' — i.e., in pure etheric perception.

The three sorts of Gold map precisely onto the three bodies of Tantric anatomy: vulgar gold is the sthula-sharira (gross body), chymical gold is the sukshma-sharira (subtle body), and divine gold is the karana-sharira (causal body). Sowing gold in gold is the principle that only like can generate like — a statement of the hermetic axiom that the spirit must be 'fixed' in a body of its own kind (the 'glorious spirit will not appear save in a body of his own kind'), which is the doctrine of the daimon: the personal daimon can only manifest through a pneuma that has been sufficiently purified to resonate at its frequency. The 'Theosophists persuaded by exact diet and certain forms of prayers to obtain the Angel of the Sun' describes precisely the theurgic practice of the PGM (the 'Solar Invocation,' PGM III.494-611) and the planetary angelic invocation that Steiner describes in 'How to Know Higher Worlds.' The Angel of the Sun is the solar daimon — the Helios-daimon that is the conduit of the soul's ascent through the planetary spheres.

gaps: The discussions of the four elements, the vegetable spirit, and the 'key of Art' need fuller extraction
The practical instructions on the 'sweetness of fire and air' and the 'exaltation of the sovereign spirit' need further treatment

issues.title: Passages are extracts, not full verbatim text
issues.description: Current passages excerpt key sections. Full text requires line-by-line sourcing.

versions.summary: Initial RO created from The Glory of Light MS. Six passages cover: Urim and Thummim, the two Lights, and the three Golds, with commentary connecting to the luminous body, Tantric chakras, and solar daimon theurgy.

timeline.event: RO created in draft state from The Glory of Light
